REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC. AND PARAGON REAL ESTATE GROUP'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER TO THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS OVERRULED. THE COMPLAINT PROVIDES A LIST OF THE UNDISCLOSED DEFECTS, SOME OF WHICH APPEAR TO HAVE BEEN LATENT DEFECTS THAT COULD HAVE BEEN OBSERVED (E.G. THE SHAPE OF THE WALLS, THE LARGE OPENINGS IN THE WALLS, ETC.) IN 2012 AND SOME OF WHICH WERE NOT OBSERVED UNTIL THE WALLS, FLOORS AND CEILINGS WERE OPENED DURING THE RENOVATION IN 2021. THE DELAYED DISCOVERY OF THOSE PATENT DEFECTS IS SUFFICIENTLY STATED AND DEMURRER CANNOT BE SUSTAINED TO A PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. THEREFORE, THE DEMURRER ON THESE GROUNDS IS OVERRULED. DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS (NOT CONCLUSIONS) TO SUPPORT EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. THESE FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS SHOULD INCLUDE (BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO) THE KNOWLEDGE OF DEFECTS BY MS. MUSCAT; THE SPECIFICS OF MISREPRESENTATIONS MADE (WHO, TO WHOM, IN WHAT MANNER, THE STATEMENT MADE) AND FACTS SUPPORTING RELIANCE ON THESE MISREPRESENTATIONS; FACTS SHOWING THAT PARAGON AND COMPASS KNEW OR SHOULD HAVE KNOWN ABOUT MS. MUSCAT'S MISREPRESENTATIONS, CONCEALMENT, FAILURE TO DISCLOSE; THE CONTRACTUAL PROVISIONS BREACHED - PLAINTIFF MAY ATTACH A COPY OF THE CONTRACT AND IDENTIFY THE BREACHED PARAGRAPHS OR QUOTE THE PARAGRAPHS IN THE BODY OF THE COMPLAINT. OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH SFRL 2.7B. FAILURE TO COMPLY IN THE FUTURE WILL RESULT IN MOTION BEING TREATED AS UNOPPOSED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC. AND PARAGON REAL ESTATE GROUP'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the operative complaint based on the statute of limitations is overruled. The complaint provides a list of the undisclosed defects, some of which appear to have been latent defects that could have been observed (e.g. the shape of the walls, the large openings in the walls, etc.) in 2012 and some of which were not observed until the walls, floors and ceilings were opened during the renovation in 2021. The delayed discovery of those patent defects is sufficiently stated and demurrer cannot be sustained to a part of a cause of action. Therefore, the demurrer on these grounds is overruled.
Demurrer is otherwise sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions) to support each element of each cause of action. These factual allegations should include (but are not limited to) the knowledge of defects by Ms. Muscat; the specifics of misrepresentations made (who, to whom, in what manner, the statement made) and facts supporting reliance on these misrepresentations; facts showing that Paragon and Compass knew or should have known about Ms. Muscat's misrepresentations, concealment, failure to disclose; the contractual provisions breached - Plaintiff may attach a copy of the contract and identify the breached paragraphs or quote the paragraphs in the body of the complaint. Opposing party failed to comply with SFRL 2.7B. Failure to comply in the future will result in motion being treated as unopposed.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
